query: recent studies on stem cell therapy for acute kidney injury

retrieved_docs.title: Rapidly Progressive Glomerulonephritis -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials
retrieved_docs.content: Finerenone  is a first-in-class selective nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist approved by the Food and Drug Administration for treating patients with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease. This medication has been shown to reduce cardiovascular mortality and proteinuria. Although preclinical animal studies suggest its effectiveness in slowing the progression of RPGN, further clinical studies are required to confirm these findings. [38] [39] Other novel therapies being studied for possible use include eculizumab and stem cell therapy. [31]

retrieved_docs.title: Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation in Sickle Cell Disease -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Although hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) remains the only available curative therapy for SCD, not all affected individuals are eligible for such therapy, primarily because of the significant toxicities associated with the procedure. HSCT is currently indicated in persons with severe SCD with complications including stroke, acute chest syndrome, recurrent pain crisis, nephropathy, retinopathy, osteonecrosis of multiple joints, and priapism. [6] The decision to proceed with HSCT is complex and must include a discussion of potential benefits and risks, such as infection, graft-versus-host disease (GVHD), and organ injury from the conditioning regimen. In addition, HSCT can have unpredictable effects on preexisting heart, lung, and kidney diseases. Transplantation is only indicated when the benefits outweigh these risks. [8] [9] [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Perioperative Acute Kidney Injury -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Innovations in laboratory analysis of AKI have determined the existence of AKI biomarkers that can be detected before renal mass is lost. [9] These biomarkers are surrogates of early stress on the renal tubules. They are mobilized secondary to cardiopulmonary bypass and surgical stimulation and reflect underlying mechanisms of renal injury since different biomarkers are released in response to specific insults. Of note, insults due to G1-phase cell cycle arrest are associated with tissue-inhibitor of metalloproteinase-2 (TIMP-2) and insulin-like growth factor-binding protein-7 (IGFBP-7) biomarkers and are predictive of major adverse kidney events, including death, persistent renal dysfunction, and need for dialysis. [28] [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor–Associated Acute Kidney Injury -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: The role of the immune system in cancer pathogenesis has been recognized since the late 19th century when inactivated bacteria were found to influence tumor progression. Currently, several antineoplastic immunotherapies are available, including ICIs, tumor vaccines, cellular immunotherapies, and immunomodulatory drugs targeting T cells. Although chemotherapy and radiation remain the cornerstone of cancer treatment, ICIs have become the first-line therapy for various tumors. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Perioperative Acute Kidney Injury -- Introduction -- Kidney Disease Improving Global Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Perioperative acute kidney injury (AKI) is a serious yet underrecognized problem in patients who have recently undergone surgery. Due to increasing age and number of comorbidities, perioperative AKI is increasing in incidence and has significant associated morbidity and mortality. [3] Postoperative AKI raises specific concerns as it elevates the risk of short- and long-term mortality, escalates hospitalization costs, and substantially increases resource utilization compared to patients without postoperative AKI. Early recognition of AKI and implementation of early goal-directed therapy is critical to reducing the incidence of progression to chronic kidney disease, renal replacement therapies (RRT), and death. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Perioperative Acute Kidney Injury -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: There are a few experimental strategies to prevent perioperative AKI, such as remote ischemic preconditioning, in which brief periods of nonlethal ischemia are given to the peripheries to prepare them for the long and lethal episodes of ischemia from the surgery. [42] Remote ischemic preconditioning involves the application of short intervals of ischemia and reperfusion of peripheral tissues or organs, resulting in the activation of protective responses of remote organ systems. For instance, inflating a blood pressure cuff on the upper arm to 200 to 300 mm Hg for 5 minutes and then releasing the pressure, followed by several repeat cycles. The threshold of including remote ischemic preconditioning in routine clinical practice is relatively low because of the noninvasive nature, unknown adverse effects, and favorable initial findings.

retrieved_docs.title: C3 Glomerulopathy -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Patients who fail to respond to therapies are encouraged to enroll in clinical trials. Novel emerging therapies targeting various regulators of complement activation are being investigated in phase 2 and 3 clinical trials. [5] Some other agents studied have included danicopan, gene therapy, and stem cell treatment, but none of these have been proven effective at this time. [5] [20] [19] [21]

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Kidney Injury -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the risk factors and clinical indicators associated with acute kidney injury to facilitate early recognition and intervention. Differentiate between prerenal, intrinsic renal, and postrenal causes of acute kidney injury, utilizing diagnostic tools and clinical assessment to guide appropriate treatment strategies. Implement evidence-based guidelines for preventing and managing acute kidney injury, including optimizing fluid resuscitation, adjusting medications, and addressing underlying causes. Communicate effectively with patients and their families, providing clear explanations of acute kidney injury, its causes, and the proposed treatment plan. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Renal Tubular Necrosis -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials
retrieved_docs.content: Iron chelators have also been studied but found less effective than the previously mentioned drugs. [30] Over 1000 ferroptosis regulators are being studied, and a full database can be accessed at http://www.zhounan.org/ferrdb/current/ . Animal studies have shown that small molecules can target ferroptosis in animal models. One such molecule is Nec-1f, which targets receptor-interacting protein kinase 1 and ferroptosis in cell lines. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Renal Tubular Necrosis -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the ischemic, nephrotoxic, and multifactorial causes of acute tubular necrosis, including high-risk scenarios such as sepsis, surgery, and exposure to nephrotoxic agents. Assess the relevance of cellular injury pathways—particularly ferroptosis, apoptosis, and necroptosis—in the progression and recovery of acute tubular necrosis. Apply guideline-recommended strategies for early fluid resuscitation, hemodynamic stabilization, and nephrotoxin avoidance in those with acute tubular necrosis to reduce renal injury severity. Coordinate transitional care and outpatient follow-up with the interprofessional healthcare team to monitor renal function, mitigate long-term complications, and reduce the risk of chronic and end-stage renal disease progression. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Renal Tubular Necrosis -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials
retrieved_docs.content: Another animal study showed that hypoxia-inducible factor 1α helps upregulate glutathione peroxidase 4, which helps decrease the accumulation of lipid peroxides, thereby decreasing ferroptosis. [43] Much attention has been paid to using antioxidants since a large part of renal damage is attributed to damage from reactive oxygen species. One of these is oleanolic acid paired with liposomes for solubility. In vitro and in vivo studies demonstrate that the compound reduces inflammation and reactive oxygen species while exhibiting antioxidant and antiapoptotic properties. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor–Associated Acute Kidney Injury -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs), which target the cytotoxic T-lymphocyte–associated antigen 4 (CTLA-4), programmed death-1 (PD-1), programmed death–ligand-1 (PD-L1), and lymphocyte activation gene-3 ( LAG-3 ) pathways, have revolutionized cancer treatment. In 2011, the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved ipilimumab (the first ICI) for the treatment of advanced melanoma. Since then, the number of approved ICIs and their indications have increased exponentially, demonstrating the extraordinary potential of these drugs in treating various tumors. Currently, 11 ICIs are approved for nearly 20 types of cancer, including melanoma, lung cancer, renal cell carcinoma, and endometrial cancer, with indications continuing to expand over time. [1]

retrieved_docs.content: Electrolyte abnormality related to chronic kidney disease (CKD) and AKI

retrieved_docs.title: Renal Biopsy -- Indications -- Non-Targeted Biopsies for Differentiating Causes of Acute Kidney Injury or Chronic Kidney Disease
retrieved_docs.content: Non-targeted biopsies are performed to differentiate etiologies of acute kidney injury or chronic kidney disease in patients with the potential for recoverable renal function, as the treatment approach may vary depending on the underlying etiology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 4 -- Community 2888
retrieved_docs.content: Acute kidney failure (فشل كلوي حاد) is clinically associated with anemia (فقر الدم), as the sudden loss of renal function often leads to reduced production of erythropoietin and impaired clearance of uremic toxins, which together contribute to a decrease in red blood cell count. This relationship highlights the importance of monitoring hemoglobin levels in patients with acute renal injury and addressing anemia promptly to improve oxygen delivery, reduce cardiovascular strain, and support overall recovery. Effective management of both conditions is essential for optimizing patient outcomes in the setting of acute kidney dysfunction.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1995
retrieved_docs.content: Dehydration (التجفاف) is clinically associated with the development of acute kidney injury (فشل كلوي حاد). In turn, acute kidney injury is clinically linked to low blood volume (قلة حجم الدم), reflecting the importance of adequate intravascular volume in preserving renal function. Acute kidney injury also encompasses the condition of extracellular blood nitrogen (نيتروجينية الدم خارج الكلوية), indicating that disturbances in nitrogenous waste handling outside the kidneys are a relevant component of its clinical spectrum. Understanding these interconnections—how dehydration can precipitate acute kidney injury, how hypovolemia contributes to its progression, and how extracellular nitrogen abnormalities manifest—helps clinicians identify risk factors, monitor patients, and implement timely interventions to protect renal function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 2 -- Community 674
retrieved_docs.content: framework that connects renal replacement therapies, pharmacologic agents, etiologic factors (e.g., hypertension, vascular injury, autoimmunity), diverse glomerular and tubular pathologies, and standardized coding structures, thereby supporting accurate diagnosis, coding, and coordinated patient‑centered management in both adult and pediatric nephrology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 5 -- Community 2962
retrieved_docs.content: Acute kidney failure (فشل كلوي حاد) is clinically associated with type 2 diabetes mellitus (مرض السكري نوع 2) and with reduced blood volume (قلة حجم الدم). Type 2 diabetes can predispose patients to acute kidney injury through chronic hyperglycemia‑induced vascular and tubular damage, making the kidneys more vulnerable to hemodynamic stress. Conversely, hypovolemia decreases renal perfusion and can directly precipitate acute kidney failure. Recognizing that both type 2 diabetes and low intravascular volume are important clinical contributors to acute kidney failure guides clinicians to monitor diabetic patients closely, ensure adequate hydration, and intervene promptly to preserve renal function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: Failure (biologic function) is associated with الفَشَلٌ الكَبِدِيُّ الكُلْوِيّ, indicating that a general loss of biological function can be linked to hepatorenal failure—a severe condition in which both liver and kidney functions are compromised. Recognizing this association is important for clinicians, as it highlights the need to assess and manage systemic organ failure comprehensively in affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2503
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is a specific, severe form of التهاب كبيبات الكلى (glomerulonephritis) and is directly linked to متلازمة رئوية كلوية (pulmonary‑renal syndrome). In the broader context, التهاب كبيبات الكلى is clinically associated with قصور كلوي مزمن (chronic renal failure), indicating that patients with this type of glomerular inflammation are at risk for progressive loss of kidney function. Recognizing the relationship between necrotizing glomerulonephritis, pulmonary‑renal syndrome, and the potential development of chronic renal failure is essential for timely diagnosis, targeted therapy, and comprehensive patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1810
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is a severe, necrotizing form of التهاب كبيبات الكلى (glomerulonephritis) that is classified under اعتلال كبيبات الكلى (renal glomerular disease). It is closely associated with داء مضاد للغشاء القاعدي الكبيبي (anti‑glomerular basement membrane disease), a condition that can trigger the immune‑mediated injury of the glomerular basement membrane. Clinically, necrotizing glomerulonephritis often presents as متلازمة رئوية كلوية (pulmonary‑renal syndrome), reflecting simultaneous involvement of the lungs and kidneys. Within the broader category of اعتلال كبيبات الكلى, other serious sequelae such as اليوريمية (uremia) are also recognized as child conditions, underscoring the potential for progressive renal dysfunction when glomerular pathology is present. Understanding these interrelationships is essential for accurate diagnosis, targeted therapy, and comprehensive management of patients with necrotizing glomerulonephritis and related glomerular disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 1433
retrieved_docs.content: and الزراعة الكلى (kidney transplantation). Major therapeutic categories, including المجازة القلبية الرئوية (cardiopulmonary resuscitation) and المعالجة بالأدوية (drug therapy), accept all four qualifiers, underscoring the integration of ethical considerations, methodological standards, quantitative evidence, and trend monitoring across patient care. Collectively, this framework provides a unified structure for ethical analysis, methodological reporting, quantitative evaluation, and trend identification, supporting comprehensive medical understanding and informed clinical decision‑making.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 2686
retrieved_docs.content: Acute kidney injury, acidosis, hyperkalemia, and extrarenal blood nitrogen are closely interrelated. Acute kidney injury is clinically associated with both acidosis and hyperkalemia, indicating that patients with sudden loss of renal function frequently develop metabolic acidosis and elevated serum potassium levels. In addition, acidosis and hyperkalemia commonly co‑occur, reflecting shared pathophysiological mechanisms in renal failure. Acute kidney injury also includes the condition termed extrarenal blood nitrogen, a manifestation of nitrogenous waste accumulation outside the kidneys. Recognizing these linked abnormalities is essential for timely diagnosis and comprehensive management of patients with acute kidney injury.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2716
retrieved_docs.content: التجفاف (dehydration) and حالة نفسية غير طبيعية (abnormal psychological state) are clinically associated with فشل كلوي حاد (acute kidney failure). Dehydration can reduce renal perfusion and precipitate acute renal injury, while an abnormal psychological condition may contribute to the development or worsening of acute kidney failure through mechanisms such as medication misuse, poor self‑care, or stress‑related hemodynamic changes. Recognizing these associations helps clinicians identify at‑risk patients early, implement preventive measures, and tailor management strategies for those presenting with dehydration or mental health disturbances to reduce the likelihood of acute kidney failure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2623
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is closely linked to داء مضاد للغشاء القاعدي الكبيبي, an anti‑basement membrane disease of the kidney, and it also forms a key component of متلازمة رئية كلوية, a pulmonary‑renal syndrome that involves simultaneous injury to the lungs and kidneys. This interrelationship highlights the importance of recognizing necrotizing glomerulonephritis not only as a severe renal pathology but also as a manifestation of systemic autoimmune processes that can affect multiple organ systems, guiding comprehensive diagnostic evaluation and coordinated therapeutic strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2177
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is a severe, necrotizing form of التهاب كبيبات الكلى (glomerulonephritis), representing a specific subtype within the broader category of glomerular inflammation. This disease is closely associated with متلازمة رئوية كلوية (pulmonary‑renal syndrome), a clinical entity in which acute lung involvement—often pulmonary hemorrhage—occurs together with rapid deterioration of renal function. Understanding that necrotizing glomerulonephritis both belongs to the spectrum of glomerulonephritis and can manifest as part of a pulmonary‑renal syndrome is essential for timely diagnosis, comprehensive evaluation of both kidney and lung pathology, and the implementation of appropriate immunosuppressive and supportive therapies to improve patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 2 -- Community 821
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is classified as a subtype of اعتلال كبيبات الكلى (glomerular disease) and therefore falls under the broader category of kidney glomerular pathology. It is closely associated with داء مضاد للغشاء القاعدي الكبيبي (anti‑basement membrane disease), reflecting a pathogenic link to auto‑immune injury of the glomerular basement membrane. In clinical practice, necrotizing glomerulonephritis often presents as part of متلازمة رئوية كلوية (pulmonary‑renal syndrome), indicating simultaneous involvement of the lungs and kidneys. The progression of اعتلال كبيبات الكلى can lead to اليوريمية (uremia), underscoring the potential for severe renal failure if the underlying glomerular injury is not promptly recognized and treated. Understanding these interconnections is essential for accurate diagnosis, risk assessment, and coordinated management of patients with necrotizing glomerulonephritis and related systemic manifestations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 1 -- Community 102
retrieved_docs.content: of acute renal failure (الفشل الكلوي الحاد). Together, these interconnections illustrate how hemodialysis, its acute and chronic complications, related comorbidities, specific glomerular pathologies, systemic autoimmune or drug‑induced syndromes, and targeted ultrasonographic evaluation of the urinary system are integral to understanding renal pathology and guiding comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 4 -- Community 2863
retrieved_docs.content: Acute kidney failure (فشل كلوي حاد) is clinically associated with dehydration (التجفاف) and with abnormal mental states (حالة نفسية غير طبيعية). When renal function declines sharply, the condition can give rise to extrarenal blood nitrogen accumulation (نيتروجينية الدم خارج الكلوية), reflecting the buildup of nitrogenous waste products that the kidneys are no longer able to clear. These interrelated clinical features highlight the importance of recognizing fluid deficits and neuropsychiatric changes as potential indicators of acute kidney injury and of monitoring nitrogen waste levels to guide timely management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1644
retrieved_docs.content: Fanconi syndrome, other diseases of the kidney and ureters, and end‑stage renal disease are clinically associated with one another. Fanconi syndrome is linked to end‑stage renal disease, indicating that patients with this tubular disorder may progress to or coexist with advanced kidney failure. In addition, Fanconi syndrome is clinically associated with other kidney and ureter diseases, suggesting frequent co‑occurrence with a broader spectrum of renal pathology. Likewise, other diseases of the kidney and ureters are associated with end‑stage renal disease, reflecting their potential to culminate in severe loss of renal function. Together, these interconnections highlight a network of renal conditions that often coexist and may collectively contribute to the development of end‑stage renal disease, underscoring the importance of comprehensive evaluation and management of kidney‑related disorders.
